---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from scipy import stats
import statsmodels.api as sm
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, classification_report, roc_auc_score, roc_curve
import warnings

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# In[ ]:


# Load dataset

df = pd.read_csv("data/macro_features.csv", parse_dates=["date"]).set_index("date")

print("Dataset shape:", df.shape)
print("Date range:", df.index.min(), "to", df.index.max())

y = df["unrate"]
print(f"\nTarget: UNRATE (Unemployment Rate)")
print(f"Mean: {y.mean():.2f}%")
print(f"Range: {y.min():.2f}% - {y.max():.2f}%")

# In[4]:


# Select candidate features for predicting unemployment rate
# Remove unrate_momentum and unrate_change to avoid data leakage
# removing these 2 as we can't use
# unemployment-derived features to predict unemployment itself)
candidate_features = [
    "termspread",
    "termspread_lag3",
    "indpro_growth",
    "indpro_momentum",
    "cpi_yoy",
    "tb3ms_lag6",
    "gs10_lag6",
]

X = df[candidate_features].copy()
print(f"Using {len(candidate_features)} candidate features")


# In[5]:


# Forward Selection:using AIC criterion for Gamma Regression
def forward_selection(X, y, p_threshold=0.10):
    remaining = list(X.columns)
    selected = []

    # Null model uses intercept only (baseline for comparison)
    X_const = sm.add_constant(pd.DataFrame(index=X.index))
    null_model = sm.GLM(y, X_const, family=sm.families.Gamma()).fit()
    current_aic = null_model.aic

    print("FORWARD SELECTION (AIC Criterion)")
    print(f"Step 0: Null model | AIC = {current_aic:.2f}")

    step = 0
    while remaining:
        step += 1
        results = []

        # one at a time for each remaining var
        for candidate in remaining:
            features = selected + [candidate]
            X_try = sm.add_constant(X[features])
            try:
                model = sm.GLM(y, X_try, family=sm.families.Gamma()).fit()
                p_val = model.pvalues[candidate]
                results.append((candidate, model.aic, p_val))
            except:
                results.append((candidate, np.inf, 1.0))

        results.sort(key=lambda x: x[1])
        best_candidate, best_aic, best_pval = results[0]

        if best_aic < current_aic and best_pval < p_threshold:
            selected.append(best_candidate)
            remaining.remove(best_candidate)
            current_aic = best_aic
            print(f"Step {step}: Add '{best_candidate}' | AIC = {best_aic:.2f} | p = {best_pval:.4f}")
        else:
            print(f"Step {step}: No improvement. Stop.")
            break

    print(f"\nSelected {len(selected)} features: {selected}")
    return selected


# In[6]:


# Run forward selection to find best features
selected_features = forward_selection(X, y)

# In[7]:


# Train final logistic regression with selected features
X_final = sm.add_constant(X[selected_features])
model = sm.GLM(y, X_final, family=sm.families.Gamma()).fit()
print(model.summary())

# In[8]:


coef_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Coefficient": model.params,
    "Std Error": model.bse,
    "z-value": model.tvalues,
    "p-value": model.pvalues,
})


def sig_stars(p):
    if p < 0.001:
        return "***"
    elif p < 0.01:
        return "**"
    elif p < 0.05:
        return "*"
    else:
        return ""  # *** p<0.001, ** p<0.01, * p<0.05


coef_df["Sig"] = coef_df["p-value"].apply(sig_stars)

print("COEFFICIENT TABLE")
print(coef_df.round(4))

# In[9]:


print("MODEL FIT STATISTICS")
print(f"Deviance:            {model.deviance:.4f}")
print(f"Pearson chi2:        {model.pearson_chi2:.4f}")
print(f"Log-Likelihood:      {model.llf:.2f}")
print(f"AIC:                 {model.aic:.2f}")
print(f"BIC:                 {model.bic:.2f}")
print(f"Pseudo R² (CS):      {model.pseudo_rsquared():.4f}")

# In[10]:


fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

residuals = model.resid_pearson
fitted = model.fittedvalues

# homoscedasticity and non-linear patterns
axes[0].scatter(fitted, residuals, alpha=0.5)
axes[0].axhline(y=0, color='r', linestyle='--')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Fitted Values')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Pearson Residuals')
axes[0].set_title('Residuals vs Fitted')

stats.probplot(residuals, dist="norm", plot=axes[1])
axes[1].set_title('Q-Q Plot')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
# Left plot: check for patterns in residuals
# Right plot: Q-Q plot checks if residuals follow normal distribution


# In[11]:


print("PREDICTION FOR February 2026")

latest = X[selected_features].iloc[-1:]
latest_with_const = sm.add_constant(latest, has_constant='add')

print(f"Latest observation: {X.index[-1].strftime('%Y-%m')}")
print(f"\nFeature values:")
for feat in selected_features:
    print(f"  {feat}: {latest[feat].values[0]:.4f}")

pred_unrate = model.predict(latest_with_const).values[0]

print(f"\n>>> Predicted Unemployment Rate for February 2026: {pred_unrate:.2f}%")

# In[12]:

# Compare with OLS (Linear Regression)
print("=" * 60)
print("OLS MODEL FOR COMPARISON")
print("=" * 60)

model_ols = sm.OLS(y, X_final).fit()
print(model_ols.summary())

# In[24]:


# Model Comparison
print("MODEL COMPARISON: Gamma vs OLS")
print("=" * 60)

pred_gamma = model.predict(latest_with_const).values[0]
pred_ols = model_ols.predict(latest_with_const).values[0]

print(f"{'Metric':<25} {'Gamma':>15} {'OLS':>15}")
print("-" * 60)
print(f"{'Predicted February 2026':<25} {pred_gamma:>15.2f}% {pred_ols:>15.2f}%")

# In[26]:


# check heteroscedasticity of OLS

from statsmodels.stats.diagnostic import het_breuschpagan

bp_test = het_breuschpagan(model_ols.resid, X_final)
print(f"Breusch-Pagan p-value: {bp_test[1]:.4f}")

null_deviance = model.null_deviance
resid_deviance = model.deviance

reduction = (null_deviance - resid_deviance) / null_deviance * 100
print(f"Null deviance: {null_deviance:.2f}")
print(f"Residual deviance: {resid_deviance:.2f}")
print(f"Deviance reduction: {reduction:.1f}%")

# ======================================================
# SHAPLEY VALUE ANALYSIS
# ======================================================
print()
print("\nSHAPLEY VALUE ANALYSIS")

# Use fitted values and coefficients from Gamma model
beta = model.params

# Drop intercept for interpretability
beta_no_const = beta.drop("const")

X_no_const = X_final.drop(columns=["const"])

# Baseline prediction: mean fitted value
baseline = model.fittedvalues.mean()

# Calculate Shapley values (linear approximation)
# contribution = beta_j * (x_ij - E[x_j])
shap_values = {}

for col in X_no_const.columns:
    shap_contrib = beta_no_const[col] * (X_no_const[col] - X_no_const[col].mean())
    shap_values[col] = shap_contrib.mean()

shap_df = pd.DataFrame.from_dict(
    shap_values, orient="index", columns=["Shapley Value"]
)

# Absolute contribution
shap_df["Abs Shapley"] = shap_df["Shapley Value"].abs()

# Percentage contribution
total = shap_df["Abs Shapley"].sum()
shap_df["Percentage (%)"] = shap_df["Abs Shapley"] / total * 100

# Sort descending
shap_df = shap_df.sort_values("Percentage (%)", ascending=False)

print("\nPredictors ranked by Shapley Percentage Contribution:")
print(shap_df[["Percentage (%)"]].round(2))

# ------------------------------------------------------
# Bar Plot
# ------------------------------------------------------

plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
plt.barh(
    shap_df.index[::-1],
    shap_df["Percentage (%)"][::-1],
    alpha=0.75
)

plt.xlabel("Percentage Contribution (%)")
plt.title("Predictors' Shapley Values (Descending Importance)")
plt.grid(axis="x", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ======================================================
# MODEL PERFORMANCE: RMSE
# ======================================================
print()
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error
import numpy as np

# Use in-sample prediction
y_pred = model.predict(X_final)

rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y, y_pred))

print(f"RMSE (in-sample): {rmse:.4f}")
print()
# ======================================================
# PREDICTION VS ACTUAL PLOT
# ======================================================

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.figure(figsize=(6, 6))

plt.scatter(
    y, y_pred,
    alpha=0.4,
    s=25
)

# 45-degree reference line
min_val = min(y.min(), y_pred.min())
max_val = max(y.max(), y_pred.max())

plt.plot(
    [min_val, max_val],
    [min_val, max_val],
    linestyle="dashed",
    color="black",
    linewidth=1.2
)

plt.xlabel("Observed U-3 Unemployment Rate (%)")
plt.ylabel("Predicted U-3 Unemployment Rate (%)")
plt.title("Observed vs Predicted U-3 Unemployment Rate")

plt.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.4)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
print()
# ======================================================
# RESIDUAL DIAGNOSTICS
# ======================================================

residuals = y - y_pred

plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))

plt.scatter(
    y_pred,
    residuals,
    alpha=0.4,
    s=25
)

plt.axhline(
    y=0,
    linestyle="dashed",
    color="red"
)

plt.xlabel("Fitted Values")
plt.ylabel("Residuals")
plt.title("Residuals vs Fitted Values")

plt.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.4)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

std_resid = residuals / residuals.std()

plt.figure(figsize=(6,4))
plt.scatter(y_pred, std_resid, alpha=0.4)
plt.axhline(0, linestyle="--", color="red")
plt.axhline(2, linestyle="dotted", color="gray")
plt.axhline(-2, linestyle="dotted", color="gray")

plt.xlabel("Fitted Values")
plt.ylabel("Standardized Residuals")
plt.title("Standardized Residuals vs Fitted")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error

mae = mean_absolute_error(y, y_pred)
print(f"MAE (in-sample): {mae:.4f}")
# Last 12 months as pseudo hold-out
y_last = y[-12:]
X_last = X_final[-12:]

y_last_pred = model.predict(X_last)

rmse_last = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y_last, y_last_pred))
print(f"RMSE (last 12 months): {rmse_last:.4f}")
pred_std = residuals.std()

lower = y_pred - 1.96 * pred_std
upper = y_pred + 1.96 * pred_std
print("\nMODEL SUMMARY")
print("-" * 30)
print(f"RMSE: {rmse:.3f}")
print(f"MAE: {mae:.3f}")

```
